steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence (Quantitative) question. The passage establishes a rule: when winds are stronger than forecast, the predicted maximum electricity output is too low, meaning actual generation exceeds the prediction. The blank must complete the example for the West region by accurately reading the graph and matching the passage's logic.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'strong enough to generate about 150 thousand more MWh of electricity from wind turbines.': The graph shows additional MWh generated above the prediction. The West bar sits at approximately 150 thousand MWh above the predicted amount. This fits the passage's rule (stronger winds = more electricity than predicted) and the correct graph value. ✓
  • Choice B — 'so weak that the electricity from wind turbines was about 175 thousand MWh less than predicted.': The West bar is above zero, meaning electricity exceeded the prediction — the opposite of "weak." The direction and the value (175) are both wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — 'so weak that the electricity from wind turbines was about 150 thousand MWh less than predicted.': The direction is wrong. The West bar is above zero, indicating more electricity than predicted, not less. ✗
  • Choice D — 'strong enough to generate about 175 thousand more MWh of electricity from wind turbines.': The direction (stronger winds, more MWh) is correct, but the value 175 misreads the graph. The West bar is approximately 150 thousand, not 175 thousand. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
